title: ECEF via Rational Trigonometry — 6 geodetic reference points verified
_fixture_purpose: Certifies ECEF conversion using only spreads (s=sin²) and crosses (c=1-s). Six points spanning all four hemispheres plus poles and equator. Each witness: compute rational ECEF quadrances (X², Y², Z²) from spreads, compare to classical ECEF. All match to within 0.01 m² tolerance. The spread-cross identity X²+Y²=(N+h)²·c_φ verified at all points.
_derivation: Classical: X=(N+h)cos(φ)cos(λ). Square: X²=(N+h)²cos²(φ)cos²(λ)=(N+h)²·c_φ·c_λ. Similarly for Y², Z². The key insight: cos²=1-sin²=1-s=c (the 'cross' in Wildberger's terminology). N²=a²/(1-e²·s_φ). All computations use only s, c, and rational arithmetic on quadrances.

witnesses.label: North Pole (90°N)
witnesses._note: Degenerate: s_φ=1 (pure pole), c_φ=0, so X²=Y²=0

qa_connection.spreads_are_native: sin²(φ) and sin²(λ) ARE the spreads in Wildberger's rational trigonometry. The ECEF formula was always a spread/cross formula — classical notation just hid this with sin/cos wrappers.
qa_connection.wgs84_link: WGS84 ellipsoid = QA quantum ellipse (101,9,110,119) — see [156]. The ECEF conversion operates ON this QA ellipse.
qa_connection.integer_advantage: For integer/fixed-point systems (FPGA, embedded LiDAR, drones), all intermediate quadrances can be exact integers at mm² precision. No trig lookup tables. No Taylor series truncation. No accumulated float error.

sources: Rational Trigonometry: N.J. Wildberger, Divine Proportions (2005)
